question: Is the original spreadsheet content on the laptop screen completely replaced with the blue, tech-inspired UI from the donor image?
Are specific recognizable features from the donor image (e.g., the central globe, 'DATA CORE ACTIVE' text, or 'Neural Network Status' panels) visible on the screen?
Are the bright diagonal sunlight glare strips (from the venetian blinds) preserved and visible on the screen area?
Does the sunlight glare appear to be overlaid on top of the new UI (i.e., the UI is visible but semi-transparent/washed out beneath the glare)?
Do the glare strips maintain the exact diagonal angle, width, and intensity as seen in the original base image?
Is the new UI content correctly perspective-warped to match the tilt and trapezoidal shape of the laptop screen?
Are the edges of the new UI content cleanly aligned with the screen bezels, without spilling onto the laptop frame or keyboard?
Is the reflection of the screen on the glass table surface consistent with the new blue UI content (rather than showing the old white spreadsheet)?
Is the laptop hardware (keyboard, trackpad, and bezels) preserved without distortion or color bleeding?
Are the background elements (venetian blinds, office chairs, plants, and windows) completely unchanged?
Is the image free from ghosting artifacts (e.g., faint remnants of the spreadsheet grid) or visible seams around the screen?
Does the overall image maintain photographic authenticity, with the new screen content matching the noise and resolution of the original photo?
